data.title: Credit Stress May Impact Bitcoin and Ethereum Before FOMC Meeting
data.description: Five major private credit fund managers have restricted investor withdrawals since late February. According to NS3.AI, this development could exert pressure on Bitcoin and Ethereum ahead of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting scheduled for March 17–18. The article highlights Cliffwater's 7% redemption limit and a record 11.5 million put contracts on major U.S. credit ETFs as indicators of increasing credit stress.

data.title: Africa's Health Nonprofits Seek Local Support After USAID Exit
data.description:
A year after the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) concluded its support, African health nonprofits are increasingly relying on local donors and innovative financing strategies to address funding shortfalls. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in funding dynamics as these organizations adapt to the absence of USAID's financial assistance.

The transition has prompted nonprofits to explore diverse funding avenues, including partnerships with local businesses and community-based fundraising efforts. This strategic pivot aims to ensure the sustainability of health programs across the continent, which have historically depended on international aid.

As these organizations navigate the evolving financial landscape, they are also focusing on building resilience and self-reliance. By fostering local support, African health nonprofits are working to maintain and expand their services, ensuring continued access to essential healthcare for communities in need.

data.title: Freedom of the Seas Threatened by Conflict in Iran
data.description: The longstanding role of the UK and US in ensuring maritime safety for global commerce is under threat due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the freedom of the seas, a principle upheld for centuries, is becoming a casualty of this war. The situation raises significant implications for international trade and security, as the stability of crucial shipping routes is jeopardized. The conflict's impact on maritime operations underscores the need for diplomatic efforts to restore peace and maintain open sea lanes for global commerce.

data.title: EU Diplomat Warns of U.S. Intentions to Divide Europe
data.description: The European Union's High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy, Kallas, has issued a stark warning regarding the United States' intentions towards Europe. According to Jin10, Kallas emphasized that everyone should be aware of the U.S.'s clear stance on wanting to divide Europe. This statement highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions and the complex dynamics between the EU and the U.S. as they navigate international relations. Kallas's remarks underscore the importance of unity within Europe in the face of external pressures.

data.title: White House Faces Challenges in Controlling Oil Price Surge
data.description: The White House is encountering difficulties in finding effective measures to address the escalating oil prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the limited options available to curb the rising costs. As global demand continues to outpace supply, the administration is under pressure to find solutions that can stabilize the market and alleviate the economic impact on consumers. The situation remains a significant concern as energy prices influence broader economic conditions.

data.title: Thailand Open to Purchasing Russian Oil, Says Deputy Prime Minister
data.description: Thailand's Deputy Prime Minister has expressed openness to the idea of purchasing oil from Russia. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and fluctuating global oil prices. The Deputy Prime Minister highlighted the importance of diversifying energy sources to ensure stability and meet the country's energy demands. This move could potentially strengthen Thailand's energy security and economic resilience. The decision aligns with Thailand's broader strategy to explore various international partnerships and trade opportunities in the energy sector.

data.title: Malaysia's Subsidy Costs Rise Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Malaysia's subsidy expenses are set to increase due to escalating oil prices driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, according to Second Finance Minister Amir Hamzah Azizan. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on global oil markets. The minister emphasized that the rising costs are a direct consequence of the instability in the region, which has led to fluctuations in oil prices. This development poses challenges for Malaysia's financial planning, as the government must allocate additional funds to manage the subsidy bill effectively. The situation underscores the broader economic implications of regional conflicts on countries reliant on oil imports.

data.title: BitMart's "BM Discover" section launches LIFE
data.description: BitMart's "BM Discovery" section listed Life If... (LIFE) on March 13th at 12:30 PM (UTC+8), and the LIFE/USDT trading pair was launched. Life If... is a community-driven meme token deployed on Solana and currently primarily traded on PumpSwap.

data.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady Near $71,300 Amid Market Consolidation
data.description: Bitcoin maintained its position around $71,300 on Friday, as reported by NS3.AI. The overall cryptocurrency market capitalization remained stable at approximately $2.4 trillion. The market is currently experiencing a consolidation phase, with Bitcoin trading near the upper end of its recent range.

data.title: The U.S. Senate Majority Leader said the Clarity Act is not expected to advance before April.
data.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune stated that the Clarity Act, a bill aimed at establishing a comprehensive regulatory framework for digital assets in the U.S., is not expected to pass the Senate Banking Committee before April. The bill, which aims to create a comprehensive regulatory framework for digital assets, has been pushed forward by the House of Representatives, but Senate debate continues. A major obstacle to its progress is the issue of stablecoin yields, with the crypto industry and the banking sector yet to reach a compromise. The banking industry believes that allowing stablecoins to offer yields could lead to an outflow of deposits from traditional institutions, while Patrick Witt, executive director of the White House Presidential Advisory Committee on Digital Assets, stated this week that compliant stablecoins could attract new global capital to the U.S. banking system. Additionally, the Senate passed a comprehensive housing bill that includes provisions prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing CBDCs; this bill will be sent to the House for further consideration and a vote. Currently, lawmakers are prioritizing President Trump's SAVE America Act, which Thune indicated the Senate will vote on next week. JPMorgan analysts previously described the potential passage of crypto market structure legislation as a "positive catalyst" for the industry in the second half of the year. Trump also stated earlier this month on Truth Social that passing the Clarity Act is "the next step in getting things done" after the GENIUS Act.

data.title: France's Far Right Faces Internal Struggles Amid Presidential Ambitions
data.description: Marine Le Pen's efforts to align France's far-right party with the nation's corporate and old-money elite are causing internal discord. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing tensions within the party as it approaches a potential presidency. Le Pen's strategy aims to broaden the party's appeal beyond its traditional base, seeking support from influential sectors of society. This shift has sparked debate among party members, some of whom are concerned about compromising core values. As the party gains traction in the political landscape, these internal challenges could impact its future direction and success in upcoming elections.

data.title: PayPay Considers Dual Listing After Successful New York IPO
data.description: PayPay, a payments app backed by SoftBank, is contemplating a dual listing following its successful $880 million trading debut in New York. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the IPO marks the largest by a Japanese company in the U.S. in a decade. The company's CEO expressed openness to exploring additional listing opportunities, aiming to expand its market presence further. PayPay's debut underscores the growing interest in fintech solutions and the potential for international expansion.

data.title: HYPE Price Surge Boosts Whale's Profits to $870,000
data.description:
The price of HYPE has risen above $38, significantly increasing the profits of a whale investor identified by the address starting with 0x4f9. According to ChainCatcher, this investor's floating profit from HYPE long positions has reached $870,000, with a return rate of 88%. The current position size is approximately $9.9 million, with an average price of $34.8.

On March 9, the investor initially transferred around $500,000 to Hyperliquid, purchasing HYPE at an average price of $30.8 using 10x leverage. In the following days, the investor continued to add funds and increase their position. As of seven hours ago, the investor was still increasing their holdings near $37, raising the average price accordingly.

data.title: Hong Kong Considers Reducing Minimum Market Value for Dual-Class Shares
data.description: Hong Kong is considering a reduction in the minimum market value required for dual-class shares to debut on its exchange. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of a broader strategy to enhance the city's appeal as a leading hub for global initial public offerings. The proposal aims to attract more companies to list in Hong Kong, thereby boosting its competitive edge in the international financial markets. This initiative reflects Hong Kong's ongoing efforts to strengthen its position as a premier destination for IPOs, amidst growing competition from other financial centers.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Alibaba Introduces Mobile App for Quick OpenClaw Deployment
data.description: Alibaba has unveiled a new mobile application designed to facilitate the rapid installation and deployment of OpenClaw, an agentic AI assistant. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the intensifying competition among China's technology giants to capitalize on the growing popularity of AI solutions. This move by Alibaba marks a significant step in the ongoing race to dominate the AI market, as companies strive to offer innovative tools that cater to the increasing demand for advanced AI capabilities.

data.title: ClawHub Developers Warned of Phishing and Credential Leak Risks
data.description:
ClawHub developers are being cautioned about potential phishing and credential leak risks. According to PANews, 23pds, the Chief Information Security Officer at SlowMist Technology, highlighted concerns regarding the security of ClawHub's reliance on GitHub for one-click login. Previously, the Sha1-Hulud worm had stolen numerous GitHub credentials from developers, posing a threat of further attacks on Skills.

The attack pathway involves credential theft, granting attackers GitHub access, allowing them to log into ClawHub as developers, and subsequently publishing malicious Skills with embedded backdoors. Once users download and install these, they may execute malicious code, leading to system intrusions.

data.title: Bank of America Revises UK Interest Rate Cut Forecast to Mid-2026
data.description: Bank of America Global Research has updated its forecast for the Bank of England's interest rate cuts. According to Jin10, the new prediction suggests that the Bank of England will reduce interest rates by 25 basis points in both June and September 2026. This revision shifts the previously anticipated rate cuts from March and June of the same year. The adjustment reflects changing economic conditions and expectations for the UK's monetary policy trajectory.

data.title: Thailand's Tourism Faces Challenges Amid Decline in European Visitors
data.description: Thailand's tourism industry is expected to face setbacks due to a decrease in European visitors, according to a report by the University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce (UTCC). The decline in European tourists is attributed to various factors, including economic uncertainties and changing travel preferences. According to Jin10, the UTCC report highlights concerns over the potential impact on Thailand's economy, which heavily relies on tourism as a significant source of revenue. The report suggests that the reduction in European tourists could lead to a slowdown in the tourism sector, affecting businesses and employment within the industry. The Thai government is urged to implement strategies to attract visitors from other regions and mitigate the impact of the decline in European arrivals. Efforts to diversify the tourism market and enhance promotional activities are seen as crucial steps to sustain the industry's growth. The report emphasizes the importance of adapting to changing global travel trends and addressing challenges to maintain Thailand's position as a leading tourist destination.

data.title: Jim Bianco Warns of Iran's Threat to Oil Prices
data.description: Jim Bianco, president of Bianco Research posted on X, warns that Iran may continue threatening ships, keeping crude prices high. He argues that declaring victory won't lower gas prices, as Iran could exploit the situation, maintaining oil prices at $200+/barrel. Bianco suggests neutralizing Iran's disruption ability to reduce oil prices.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $72.3677 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $72.3677 million yesterday (March 12th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net inflow of $52.0195 million, bringing FETH's historical total net inflow to $2.385 billion. This was followed by the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net inflow of $18.6814 million, bringing ETHA's historical total net inflow to $11.949 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $11.862 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.76%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $11.719 billion.
